figures.caption: Figure 6: Experimental setup for learning physical dynamics. (A) Raw vibration signals are converted to timefrequency representation (TFR); (B) Physics-constrained model is trained of TFR; (C) Perform cross-dataset generalization tests.
Figure 7: Visualization of learned long-range nonlinear degradation trajectories by each model. (A) XJTU-SY dataset; (B) HUST dataset; (C) PRONOSTIA dataset.

Figure 2: Comparison of attention mass distribution with and without Gsink. Setup: The test is conducted on XJTUSY Bearing 1 from first operating condition with FLUID, using a 10-blocks Transformer trained for 50 epochs with full pairwise query-key concatenation and standard residual connections. Test: (A) The first 200 samples, showing the attention sink, and (B) the last 200 samples, showing the performance gains. Attention without Gsink in (A) shows a 2.43% decrease, dropping from 0.255 to 0.249 on average, while in (B) the last samples improve from 0.155 to 0.176, corresponding to a 13.5% gain. (C) Gradient norm comparison over 50 epochs, showing that including Gsink reduces initial gradient spikes and stabilizes training.

Figure 3: Intuitive reconstruction visualization of irregular spiral trajectories: (A) SDPA-Transformer; (B) ODEFormer; (C) OT-Transformer; (D) ContiFormer; (E) FLUID.

Figure 1: Illustration of the internal architecture of FLUID Transformer. Input embeddings are shared between the encoder and decoder. The encoder consists of self-LAN with Gsink and HCliquid, while the decoder consists of crossLAN and self-LAN.
